Grid.TextBlock:
JCS WASHINGTON DC//J3//
USCINCLANT NORFOLK VA
USCINCEUR VAIHINGEN GE
USCINCPAC HONOLULU HI
CINCSAC OFFUTT AFB NE

TextBlock.Text: Soviet RAIDER GROUP Operations in Indian Ocean

Grid.TextBlock:
1. Reporting indicates the Soviet surface raider group is currently transiting north toward Indian territorial waters. Moscow has secured Indian agreement for the force to enter Cochin harbor, despite U.S. diplomatic objections. Once the Soviet group reaches India, U.S. response options will be significantly constrained by India’s neutral status.
2. This Soviet raider group has proven itself to be highly resourceful and dangerous. It is responsible for multiple attacks on convoys in the area, the strike on Mauritius of 03 JUL, and yesterday's attack on Logistics Force units west of Diego Garcia which resulted in loss of USS CAMDEN (AOE-2). Further, South Africans report two of their diesel boats disappeared attempting to interdict its resupply units near Crozet Islands.
3. ENTERPRISE CVBG (CTF 71) has been reassigned to proceed southeast from Gulf of Oman to pursue the Soviet formation. However, loss of USS CAMDEN yesterday has greatly reduced sortie generation margins and limited flexibility for prolonged pursuit. ENTERPRISE air wing is operating at 50% capacity after loss of CAMDEN.
4. We assess that the most favorable opportunity to neutralize the Soviet raider group exists during its current northbound transit toward India. Entry into Indian-controlled waters will negate any possibility of military action against them. A follow on analysis will be sent with options for force composition required to keep Soviet force bottled up at Cochin, if necessary.

Distribution by ER/16 July 1985 w/Att
원문 - 각 수신자
